description_long: This object is a vintage electronic device, likely a radio or communications receiver, with a rectangular beige and white body. The front panel features a large display window with frequency markings, several labeled switches or buttons, and a translucent indicator light. The sides have black rotary knobs and a detachable metal handle, suggesting portability. The back panel is equipped with various ports, connectors, and ventilation slots, indicating its function as a complex electronic appliance. The casing appears to be made of metal and plastic, with visible screws and mounting points. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and functionality, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century electronics.
description: A beige and white vintage electronic device with a large display, control switches, side knobs, and a detachable metal handle, likely used for radio or communications.
description_view_0: Front view showing the display window with frequency markings, control switches, and indicator light.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the black rotary knob, handle attachment, and part of the control panel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying multiple ports, connectors, ventilation slots, and the full length of the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing another handle attachment and a plain side panel.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
